evidence_span: after the administration of heat-killed A. oryzae spores, the relative abundance of an anti-inflammatory Bifidobacterium pseudolongum strain became 2.0-fold greater than that of the control
notes: DSS mouse model; heat-killed spores used

evidence_span: we examined the effect of A. oryzae spore administration on the development of colitis induced by dextran sodium sulfate in mice; we found that colitis was alleviated by not only heat-killed A. oryzae spores, but also the cell wall extracted from the spores
notes: DSS-induced colitis in mice; both whole spores and cell wall extract showed alleviating effect

evidence_span: vitexin administration could significantly alleviate the signs of colitis via suppressing body weight loss, reducing disease activity index (DAI) score, and mitigating colonic damage
notes: DSS-induced UC mouse model; vitexin is a major active component from hawthorn (botanical)

evidence_span: Vitexin is beneficial for regulating abundances of some certain bacteria, such as Bacteroides
notes: DSS colitis mice model; Bacteroides listed as regulated by vitexin

evidence_span: Vitexin is beneficial for regulating abundances of some certain bacteria, such as Bacteroides, Helicobacter, Alistipes, Lachnospiraceae_NK4A136_group, and Lachnospiraceae_UCG-006
notes: DSS colitis mice; Helicobacter typically reduced in colitis treatment contexts; included as regulated taxon

evidence_span: publicly available data sets and patient stool samples showed that a subset of patients with C difficile infection harbored high levels of Fusobacterium species
notes: Direct observation in patient samples; C. difficile infection can present with dysbiosis similar to IBD-related microbiota disruption

evidence_span: LAF alleviated d-galactose-induced dysbiosis of the intestinal flora by reducing the abundance of harmful bacteria Desulfovibrio and Helicobacter
notes: d-galactose-induced aging mouse model; LAF = Lactiplantibacillus plantarum JS19-fermented goat milk

evidence_span: LAF alleviated d-galactose-induced dysbiosis of the intestinal flora by reducing the abundance of harmful bacteria Desulfovibrio and Helicobacter
notes: d-galactose-induced aging mouse model; LAF = Lactiplantibacillus plantarum JS19-fermented goat milk

evidence_span: while greatly promoting the growth of beneficial Rikenellaceae_RC9_gut_group and Eubacterium

Treatment with aqueous MR extract markedly alleviated DSS-induced colitis
MR administration also markedly modulated the gut microbiota, increasing the relative abundance of beneficial genera (Bacteroides and Alloprevotella)
notes: DSS-induced murine model; most emphasized taxa from single list

evidence_span: decreasing the abundance of the pathobiont Erysipelatoclostridium
notes: DSS-induced murine model; explicitly identified as pathobiont

evidence_span: BL@Map@Alg reduced weight loss, colon shortening, and inflammatory cytokines compared to untreated colitis
notes: Mouse DSS colitis model showing treatment effect; BL colonization demonstrated but this is an intervention outcome, not a baseline disease association.

evidence_span: we supplemented the chow diet with an anthocyanin-rich extract (RED) to evaluate disease reduction in SAMP mice and changes in fecal microbiota/metabolome. Our data reveal that 10-week supplementation with anthocyanin-rich extract ameliorated disease severity in SAMP mice
notes: SAMP mouse model of CD-like ileitis; dietary fortification with polyphenol extract

evidence_span: engineered Bacteroides thetaiotaomicron optimized to produce tryptamine (Trp D+)
notes: DSS mouse model; engineered strain explicitly produces tryptamine

evidence_span: 16S-sequencing links the presence of biofilms to a dysbiotic gut microbiome, including overgrowth of Escherichia coli and Ruminococcus gnavus
notes: Endoscopic study of 117 patients with in-depth molecular analysis; E. coli overgrowth observed in IBS patients with biofilms (57%)

evidence_span: 16S-sequencing links the presence of biofilms to a dysbiotic gut microbiome, including overgrowth of Escherichia coli and Ruminococcus gnavus
notes: Endoscopic study; R. gnavus overgrowth observed in IBS patients with biofilms

evidence_span: 16S-sequencing links the presence of biofilms to a dysbiotic gut microbiome, including overgrowth of Escherichia coli and Ruminococcus gnavus

evidence_span: 16S-sequencing links the presence of biofilms to a dysbiotic gut microbiome, including overgrowth of Escherichia coli and Ruminococcus gnavus

evidence_span: mice treated with a combination of postbiotic and paraprobiotic alongside DSS exhibited a reduction in DSS-induced negative effects on both phenotypical characteristics and molecular indices, particularly the Nrf2- and NF-kB-related genes, with a notable focus on postbiotic
notes: DSS-induced colitis mouse model; postbiotic derivative from probiotic strains

evidence_span: mice treated with a combination of postbiotic and paraprobiotic alongside DSS exhibited a reduction in DSS-induced negative effects on both phenotypical characteristics and molecular indices
notes: DSS-induced colitis mouse model; paraprobiotic derivative from probiotic strains

evidence_span: A dysbiosis of the bacterial community was observed in biopsies and stool. Results suggest Bacteroides is likely key in CD
notes: Multi-cohort observational analysis (n=63 British + n=41 Dutch); Bacteroides dysbiosis explicitly named as key to CD

evidence_span: Malassezia and Candida were increased in patients taking immunosuppressants
notes: Dutch cohort; Candida increased in CD patients; context limited to immunosuppressant-treated subset

evidence_span: Longitudinal analysis showed an increase in Cyberlindnera in relapse
notes: Fungal genus; explicitly increased during CD relapse phase

evidence_span: The results show that HLP alleviate the inflammation of UC and reduce histological damage and secretion of tumor necrosis factor-α (TNF-α), IL-6, IL-1β, and IL-10.
notes: DSS-induced UC rat model; HLP gavage intervention

evidence_span: live L. plantarum DPUL-S164 (S164) and its tryptophan metabolite, ILA, showed an effective ameliorating effect on the intestinal barrier injury of mice
notes: DSS-induced UC mouse model; explicit production of ILA by L. plantarum strain

evidence_span: S164 and its tryptophan metabolite, ILA, showed an effective ameliorating effect on the intestinal barrier injury of mice treated by antibiotic cocktail and sodium dextran sulfate
notes: DSS-induced UC mouse model; ILA (tryptophan metabolite) administered as bioactive compound intervention

evidence_span: 40% of patients with HS had a microbiota configuration similar to that of CD, characterised by the enrichment of pathogenic genera (Enterococcus, Veillonella and Escherichia_Shigella)
notes: n=55 HS, n=102 CD, n=95 controls; 40% of HS patients had CD-like microbiota enrichment

evidence_span: characterised by the enrichment of pathogenic genera (Enterococcus, Veillonella and Escherichia_Shigella) and the depletion of putatively beneficial genera (Faecalibacterium)
notes: 40% of HS patients with CD-like microbiota showed Faecalibacterium depletion

evidence_span: Lachnospira was decreased in IBD patients with cancer
notes: Observational cohort study, n=215 IBD patients; cancer prevalence 2.8%

evidence_span: Agathobacter was decreased... in UC patients with any neoplasia
notes: UC subgroup with neoplasia (dysplasia or cancer); n=106 UC patients

evidence_span: Escherichia-Shigella increased in UC patients with any neoplasia
notes: UC subgroup with neoplasia (dysplasia or cancer); n=106 UC patients

evidence_span: At week 12, 75% [9/12] of participants in the CHD had an FC >100 μg/g, vs 20% [3/15] of participants in the MDP group.
notes: RCT n=28 (15 MDP, 13 CHD), 12-week intervention, faecal calprotectin as marker of inflammation

evidence_span: the MDP induced alterations in microbial species associated with a protective role in colitis [Alistipes finegoldii and Flavonifractor plautii]
notes: RCT, emphasized as protective species in UC context
